fileContext.overview: This file provides a set of Git-related utility functions for managing branches, diffs, and content hashing. It allows for retrieving branch information, computing content hashes, and capturing the current Git state.

symbols.name: getCurrentBranch
symbols.purpose: Returns the current branch name, or HEAD commit hash if detached, or 'default' for empty repos.
symbols.behavior: Tries to get the current branch using 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', falling back to 'git rev-parse --short HEAD' if that fails, and returning 'default' if the repo is empty.

symbols.name: getHeadCommit
symbols.purpose: Returns the commit hash that HEAD points to.
symbols.behavior: Tries to get the commit hash using 'git rev-parse HEAD', returning an empty string if that fails.

symbols.name: getChangedFilesBetweenRefs
symbols.purpose: Get files that differ between two refs (branches/commits).
symbols.behavior: Uses 'git diff --name-status' to get the files that were added, modified, or deleted between the two refs.
symbols.returns: { added: string[], modified: string[], deleted: string[] }

symbols.name: getUncommittedChanges
symbols.purpose: Get uncommitted changes (both staged and unstaged) relative to HEAD.
symbols.behavior: Uses 'git diff --name-status HEAD' and 'git diff --name-status --cached' to get the modified and deleted files, and 'git ls-files -o --exclude-standard' to get the untracked files.
symbols.returns: { modified: string[], deleted: string[], untracked: string[] }

symbols.name: getAllChangedFiles
symbols.purpose: Get ALL files that have changed relative to the last indexed state.
symbols.behavior: Combines branch diff + uncommitted changes into a single set.

symbols.name: getFileContentHash
symbols.purpose: Compute a content hash for a file using the same algorithm as git (SHA-1 of blob).
symbols.behavior: Uses 'git hash-object' if in a git repo, falling back to SHA-256 of content.

symbols.name: getFileHashAtRef
symbols.purpose: Get the git blob hash for a file at a specific ref (commit/branch).
symbols.behavior: Uses 'git rev-parse' to get the hash of the file at the specified ref.

symbols.name: captureGitState
symbols.behavior: Returns an object with the current branch, head commit, and timestamp.
